w:r.w:t: Actionable improvements, prioritised by phase | Prepared April 2026

The site is a strong foundation — well-designed, premium-positioned, and built around a genuine differentiator (the in-house QC inspection platform with live webcam supervision and Garments-on-Hanger capability). For a B2B freight forwarder serving European fashion brands, it already looks the part.

That said, three critical bugs are leaking trust today (a wrong branch address, a fake services dropdown, a dead Track-Shipment button), the site is still on a developer staging URL (indolog.vercel.app) instead of indologindia.com, and several conversion mechanics that visitors expect on a 2026 logistics website (WhatsApp, inline quote form, real testimonials, GSTIN/IEC, link-preview metadata) are missing.

Fix the seven items in Phase 1 this week and you stop bleeding qualified visitors. Phase 2 (within 30 days) turns those visitors into leads. Phase 3 (within 90 days) builds the content moat that compounds over time.
Premium navy + gold + light-blue palette. Outfit + Lora pairing works well.
Freight + QC inspection is a genuine differentiator.
Homepage strong; service pages, case studies, and industries thin.
Certifications strong; GSTIN/IEC and testimonials missing.
CTAs visible but no inline form, no WhatsApp, dead tracker.
No meta description, no OG tags, JS-only render hurts crawlers.
Wrong Coimbatore address, fake dropdown links, dead tracker.
Strong foundation. Fix the bugs and the domain and it converts.
Stop the credibility leaks. Domain + bugs + SEO basics.
Turn visitors into qualified leads. Forms, WhatsApp, testimonials, analytics.
Build the content moat. Industry pages, case studies, real photography, performance.
These are the items that are actively costing you credibility right now. Most can be done in hours; none should take more than a few days. Fix them before any other work.
Coimbatore office shows a Chennai address

Under the COIMBATORE heading on the homepage, the address reads "67, Thiruvallur Street, Meenambakkam, Chennai-600027" — a Chennai pincode. Either the heading is wrong or the address is wrong, and either way it makes the entire offices section feel unreliable.
Verify the correct Coimbatore branch address with the regional manager and update the offices section. Audit every other branch entry for similar copy-paste errors.
Services dropdown links are all fake
| Effort: 1–2 days per page (6 pages) | Impact: UX + SEO

Every item in the Services dropdown (Ocean Freight, Air Freight, Road Freight, Customs Brokerage, Multimodal, Import Console) links to the same generic /services page. A user clicking "Air Freight" expects an air-freight page. You also lose six high-value SEO landing pages targeting different keywords.
Build a dedicated page for each service line. Each page should include: scope of work, process steps, transit times to major lanes, documentation handled, FAQs, and a service-specific quote CTA.
| Effort: Half day for stub, 2–3 weeks for real tracker | Impact: Credibility

The Track Shipment button links to /#track — an in-page anchor with no actual tracking functionality. A non-working tracker on a logistics website is one of the most damaging credibility hits a freight forwarder can have.

Short term: replace with a simple form that accepts AWB / B/L / Booking number, validates format, and emails ops@indologindia.com so a human can respond within 1 hour. Long term: integrate with carrier APIs (or your TMS) for live tracking.
Half the offices have no contact person
| Effort: 1 hour to collect, 30 min to publish | Impact: Conversion
Only Delhi and Chennai list a named contact and phone number. Mumbai, Kolkata, Ahmedabad, Bangalore and Coimbatore are address-only. A potential client in Mumbai literally cannot tell who to call.
Add a named contact, mobile number, and email for every branch. Format: Name (Role) · Mobile · Email · Office hours. Where appropriate, add a small headshot — humans buy from humans.
| Effort: 1–2 hours of DNS work | Impact: Trust / brand
indolog.vercel.app reads as a developer staging URL to enterprise buyers. No European fashion brand will commit a shipment quote to a .vercel.app domain. Indolog already owns indologindia.com.

Point the new build to indologindia.com (or a fresh subdomain like new.indologindia.com if you want to stage). Set up 301 redirects from any indexed vercel.app URLs and add HTTPS via Vercel's automatic SSL.

The HTML head has no meta description, no Open Graph tags, no Twitter card, and no canonical URL. When the link is shared on LinkedIn / WhatsApp / Slack, the preview is blank. Search engines have no description to display in results.

Add the following to <head>: meta description (~155 chars), og:title, og:description, og:image (1200x630 navy bg with logo), og:url, og:type=website, twitter:card=summary_large_image, and a canonical URL. Set unique meta per page.

The hero greets visitors with their detected city — for example "Serving Arcola, United States" for a visitor near Arcola, VA. While clever, naming a small town the visitor never told you about can feel surveillance-y on first contact, before any cookie consent.

Either remove the personalised greeting entirely, or downgrade to country-only and wrap it in friendlier framing — e.g. "Connecting India ↔ United States since 2012". Add a clear cookie / privacy notice.

Once Phase 1 is in place, the site is no longer leaking trust. Phase 2 is about turning the existing traffic into actual quote requests — adding the friction-removing mechanics that B2B logistics buyers expect.
Add a floating WhatsApp Business button
| Effort: 2 hours | Impact: Direct lead capture

WhatsApp is the dominant B2B channel in Indian logistics and increasingly used by overseas apparel buyers. The site currently has no WhatsApp entry point. A meaningful share of leads will not pick up a phone but will WhatsApp a number.

Add a fixed bottom-right floating WhatsApp button linking to wa.me/919266896322 with a pre-filled message: "Hi Indolog, I'd like a quote for...". Use WhatsApp Business so you get analytics, quick replies, and away-messages.
Replace generic Get-a-Quote with an inline quick-quote form
Currently the gold "Get a Quote" button takes the user to a separate /contact page — extra friction for a high-intent action. Most freight competitors now show the quote form inline on the homepage.

Add a 6-field inline quote form on the homepage hero or just below: Origin, Destination, Mode (Air/Sea/Road), Weight, Volume/CBM, Commodity, plus name + email + phone. Email the lead to ops in under 5 seconds. Show a success message and an auto-reply email.
Add real testimonials, not just logos

TWINSET Milano, MarcCain, Patrizia Pepe, PINKO are listed as brands you serve. That is good but unverifiable. A named quote from a buyer or merchandiser at one of these brands is worth more than ten unverified logos.

Reach out to your top 5 satisfied buyers and request a 2–3 sentence testimonial with name, title, company, and photo. Even one or two would dramatically lift conversion. Keep them on a rotating carousel near the QC Platform section.
Add GSTIN, IEC, CIN to the footer
| Effort: 10 minutes | Impact: Trust / due diligence
Indian B2B buyers and overseas compliance teams checking an Indian freight forwarder look for these registration numbers. Their absence makes the site look provisional.
Add GSTIN, Import Export Code (IEC), CIN, and (if applicable) AEO status, MTO licence number, and IATA agent code in the footer. Cluster them next to the logo and certification badges.
| Effort: 1 hour (with stakeholder review) | Impact: First-3-second comprehension

"Your Ultimate Logistics Partner for Seamless Success" is interchangeable with hundreds of competitor sites and tells the visitor nothing concrete. Your better lines ("Person-to-person handover — not a drop at the gate") are buried further down.

Rewrite the hero headline to be specific. Suggested direction: "Door-to-door freight + independent QC, from an Indian factory to your shop floor. One partner. One contract. Zero surprises." A/B test against a second variant focusing on QC if your buyer set is mostly fashion.
Add a proper contact page with branch maps
The contact page (per nav) is not yet rich. For a 7-office network, embedded Google Maps for each branch significantly increase legitimacy.

Add an embedded Google Map for each branch, click-to-call mobile numbers, click-to-mail emails, branch-specific email aliases (delhi@, mumbai@, etc.), and clear office hours. Add a fallback general inquiry form.
Set up analytics + heatmap from day one
Without analytics you have no idea where visitors drop off, which buttons they click, or which pages convert.

Install Plausible or GA4 plus Microsoft Clarity (free) for session recordings and heatmaps. Set conversion goals on Quote-Form-Submit, WhatsApp-Click, Phone-Click, Track-Submit. Review weekly for the first 2 months.
Add a response-time promise and form-submit confirmation
Today there is no visible promise about how fast the user will hear back. After a form submit there is also no clear confirmation flow on the page or in their inbox.

Show "We reply within 4 business hours" near every CTA. After form submission, show an in-page success state and trigger an auto-reply email (with the lead's name, the data they submitted, and the sales rep's name/photo who will follow up).

Phase 3 is about building durable content assets — pages and stories that keep working long after they ship. These take longer because they require real input from operations, sales, and clients, but they are what separate a brochure site from a sales engine.
| Effort: 1 week | Impact: SEO + lead quality
The site currently lists 5 industries (Fashion, Electronics, Pharma, Auto, Marble) but does not have a dedicated landing page per industry.

Build one page per industry with: typical lanes served, regulatory/documentation needs, packaging considerations, case study, named industry lead. Fashion deserves the deepest page given the GOH and QC moat.
| Effort: 1–2 weeks | Impact: Conversion + sales enablement
There are no case studies. Buyers comparing freight partners use case studies to validate competence and de-risk the decision.

Write 2–3 case studies (ideally 1 RMG/fashion, 1 pharma, 1 marble). Each ~600 words: client situation, challenge, Indolog solution, outcome with hard numbers (transit time, cost saving, claims rate). Include client quote and photos.
Add a News / Events / Insights page
| Effort: 2 days for setup, ongoing for content | Impact: SEO + brand
Indolog publishes regularly on LinkedIn (Intermodal Sao Paulo, hiring posts, awards) but none of this lives on the website. The site reads as static.

Add a /news or /insights section. Republish LinkedIn posts and trade-show appearances. Add 1–2 thought pieces per quarter ("Why factory-controlled QC is risky for European fashion buyers", etc.). Drives SEO and gives sales reps content to share.
Add facility, team, and operations photography
| Effort: 1 photo shoot (1 day) + edit | Impact: Trust
Imagery on the site appears to be generic stock photography. For a company with a 12,000 sq ft QC facility, GOH steel containers, and 7 branches, real photos would be far more powerful.

Commission a half-day photoshoot covering: QC facility (with the live webcam setup visible), GOH containers, warehouse, packing operations, leadership, branch teams. Replace stock images progressively.
Indolog actively hires (the LinkedIn page advertises ocean exports and import roles), but there is no careers page on the site. All hiring traffic dies on LinkedIn.
Add a /careers page with current openings, application form, life-at-Indolog photos, and benefits. Link from the footer and from any LinkedIn job posts.
Add a Privacy Policy, Terms, and Cookie Notice
| Effort: Half day with legal review | Impact: Compliance
If the site uses geo-IP to greet visitors and any analytics/cookies, a privacy policy and cookie notice are required (GDPR for EU buyers, India DPDP Act).

Publish a Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, and a cookie consent banner. Cover: what data is collected, why, how long it is kept, how to request deletion. Have these reviewed by Indian counsel given DPDP applicability.
| Effort: Half day audit, 1–2 days fixes | Impact: SEO + mobile UX

The current build loads a 475 KB JS bundle and uses several large hero images. On a 3G connection or budget Android the experience can degrade. Vercel and React are fast by default but image-heavy logistics sites often regress.

Run PageSpeed Insights and target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms. Convert images to WebP, lazy-load below-the-fold imagery, code-split routes, and pre-render the homepage HTML so SEO crawlers see real content.
Will Visitors Be Able to Connect?

The honest answer is: yes, but with leaks. The connection paths exist — phone number, email, the gold Get-a-Quote CTA on every screen. A motivated buyer will get through. But here is where you lose the less-motivated ones today.
Get-a-Quote button takes the user to a separate /contact page instead of an inline quick-quote form. Every extra click costs leads.
No WhatsApp button means a chunk of Indian SME buyers and South-East Asian agents who prefer messaging over email simply do not reach out.
No live chat or response-time promise creates doubt about whether anyone is actually monitoring the inbox.
Mumbai, Kolkata, Bangalore, Ahmedabad and Coimbatore visitors literally cannot find a person to call from your offices section.
The Track Shipment button leads nowhere, so existing customers who came to track a live shipment leave frustrated.
Once the bugs are fixed, the domain is live, every office has a contact, and a WhatsApp button + inline quick-quote form are in place, you should see two measurable changes:
Roughly 2–4× more inbound contacts from the website (industry benchmark for adding inline form + WhatsApp on a B2B freight site).
Higher quality of those contacts because the lane / mode / weight fields self-qualify the lead before sales picks it up.

Indolog has the rare combination of an actual moat (the QC platform), a real network (7 India offices, 50+ countries), and a credible brand stack (IATA / FIATA / AMTOI / MTO / JCTrans). The website does not yet do justice to that. Once the seven Phase-1 items are closed and Phase 2 is in flight, the gap between what Indolog actually delivers and what the website communicates will close, and the site will start earning its keep as a sales channel rather than a brochure.
Questions, pushback, or want me to draft any of this content (page copy, case-study templates, OG image, quote-form spec)? Happy to.
